findings.problem: The decisive betrayal beat is slightly overdetermined by the clerk and padlock appearing at the exact instant Silas finds his opening. Gideon's leverage is already concrete and sufficient in the tent scene, so the just-in-time reveal makes the turn feel more stage-managed than chosen under sustained pressure, which blunts the chapter spine's intended ordeal logic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the choice turn around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:245, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:247, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:249 so the clerk/padlock pressure is either visible before Silas starts his dirty counter-speech or removed as a fresh reveal. Keep Junie's immediate risk legible, but let the turn at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:257 read as Silas acting under already-established coercion rather than being mechanically interrupted at the live seam.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, before Silas reaches the opening at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241, the revised passage already makes Junie's immediate exposure visible enough that a reader can track the cost of refusal, and the line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:257 reads as his chosen capitulation under that pressure. Fail if the clerk/padlock first arrives only after the counter-speech has started and functions as the new deciding trigger.

findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same clipped-emphasis machinery: noun-fragment stacks and demonstrative summary tags ('Fuel arithmetic. Soup as counting.', 'Buying. With what?', 'No. Not damned.', 'Laughter. Good.', 'That was the first blade.', 'There it was...'). Once or twice these snaps work; at this frequency they flatten cadence into a recognizable tic and, in the sharpest cases, drift toward the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated emphasis beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:153, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:229, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:237, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:245, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:301, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:315, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:333 by converting several of them into fuller or differently shaped sentences that carry the same pressure without repeated fragment bursts or 'That was/There it was' labeling. Keep the force; change the sentence architecture.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised marked passages no longer rely on the same clipped-tag cadence in more than two of those sites, and each beat remains immediately legible on first read without noun-fragment stacking or demonstrative summary labels doing the interpretive work. Fail if three or more of the cited passages still use the same fragment-plus-tag construction.

summary: Strong scene embodiment, concrete domestic leverage, and differentiated character voices support the chapter. Booker fails because the coercion turn is slightly over-staged by a just-in-time clerk reveal, and prose fails because clipped fragment/tag constructions recur often enough to become a chapter-level tic; craft and dialogue otherwise clear the bar.
